title: PLA 6 Sorties + 8 PLAN Vessels Around Taiwan April 9 — 5 of 6 Cross Median Line Into Northern + SW ADIZ; KMT Delegation Travels to Beijing (Day 3)
detail: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense reported (as of 0600 local time April 9, 2026) detecting 6 PLA aircraft sorties alongside 8 PLAN vessels and 1 official ship operating around Taiwan. Of the 6 sorties, 5 crossed the Taiwan Strait median line and entered Taiwan's northern and southwestern ADIZ sectors. Taiwan deployed CAP aircraft, naval ships, and coastal missile systems in response. The April 9 count (6 sorties) represents a significant uptick from the 2-sortie level of April 8 and follows the pattern of elevated PLA air activity ahead of major KMT-PRC diplomatic events — specifically Day 3 of KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun's China visit, during which her delegation traveled from Nanjing to Beijing in preparation for an expected meeting with President Xi Jinping on April 10. The multi-sector northern and southwestern ADIZ crossings signal PLA intent to maintain visible pressure while the KMT diplomatic channel proceeds. The 8-vessel PLAN count is slightly down from 9 on April 8, but the notable surge in air activity to 6 sorties is the highest single-day count since the early-April period and significantly elevated relative to the 1–3 sortie baseline observed during April 3–8.

title: KMT Delegation Arrives in Beijing — Day 3; Xi Jinping Meeting Confirmed for April 10
detail: KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun's delegation arrived in Beijing on April 9, 2026 — Day 3 of their April 7–12 mainland China visit — completing their journey from Nanjing after the April 8 Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum tribute. The delegation's arrival in Beijing triggered confirmation from Chinese state media that a formal meeting with CCP General Secretary and President Xi Jinping would take place at the Great Hall of the People on April 10. The visit is being closely monitored by Taiwan's DPP government and US national security officials in the 35 days leading up to the scheduled Trump-Xi Beijing summit on May 14–15, 2026. CGTN and Xinhua covered the delegation's Beijing arrival with extensive state media framing. The anticipated Xi-Cheng meeting would be the highest-level CPC-KMT party-to-party exchange in approximately a decade.
